kicker: PT LEVEL I // TYPES, METHODS & WHO RUNS THEM

beats.narration: Walk up to a penetrant bench and the labels read like code. Type, method, sensitivity, developer form — let's make that language yours.
Start with the dye. Type one glows fluorescent under UV in a dark booth for top sensitivity. Type two is red, read in daylight.

payload.heading: Two dyes, two ways to look

beats.narration: Then the method — how excess comes off without stripping flaws. Four choices: water-washable, two post-emulsifiable systems, and solvent-removable for field kits.

payload.heading: Four ways to take it back off

chips.s: Emulsifier built in; easy to over-wash

beats.narration: PT lives or dies on technique, so the people are qualified too. Trainee, Level One, Level Two, Level Three — interpretation authority begins at Level Two.

payload.heading: Who is trusted to run it

beats.narration: So the label is your spec sheet. Penetrant, emulsifier, and developer are qualified as one family — never mix brands without approval.

payload.sub: Type, method, sensitivity, developer — qualified together.
